Applciation For Default Judgment And Statement Of The Case
Off Calendar Sep-17-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 514, Judge: Donald Sullivan, Clerk: Yolanda Mazariegos, CSR: Sonia Wong, #11288.

Julian T. Lastowski, Esq., appearing for Plaintiff, Fidelity National Title Insurance Company.
No appearance by defaulted defendant(s) Richard T. Rushig.
Default of Defendant(s) has been duly entered.
Court ordered Defendants Does 1-20 dismissed without prejudice.
Nancy Van Tassel sworn and examinded on behalf of Plaintiff.
After Evidence and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff and against Defaulted Defendant(s) as follows:

Damages: $115,915.30
Pre-Judgment Interest at the legal rate of 10% per annum or $31.75 per day from January 24, 2012 to August 1, 2013 in the amount of: $17,621.25
Costs: $1,690.00
Total Judgment in the amount of $135,226.25
Defendant Richard T. Rushing shall be credited with such amounts received from Defendant Ingrid K. Rushign in this matter.
Judgment signed in open court.

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-19-2012 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Julian T. Lastowski, Esq. of the Fidelity National Law Group, Tel.925.930.9550, appearing for the plaintiff and opposing party.

No other appearance. The court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT INGRID LEGGE's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is Overruled. The allegations of the complaint do not show that plaintiff's claims are necessarily time- barred. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Not reported.

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-19-2012 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Julian T. Lastowski, Esq. of the Fidelity National Law Group, Tel.925.930.9550, appearing for the plaintiff and opposing party.

No other appearance. The court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT INGRID LEGGE's MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF THE COMPLAINT is Denied. The allegations of the complaint do not show that plaintiff's claims are necessarily time-barred. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Not reported.
